calculated_metrics.confidence_note: Cost per outcome calculation is not available. This organisation conducts significant multi-domain work (e.g., human education, environmental conservation). Dividing the total budget solely by quantified animal outcomes would artificially inflate the cost and misrepresent their financial efficiency.

details.formula: Calculation aborted due to multi-domain operations.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

calculated_metrics.confidence_note: IES is a Back-of-the-Envelope Calculation (BOTEC) to standardize impact across interventions and species. It relies on philosophical and empirical assumptions.

calculated_metrics.name: Impact Equivalency Score (IES)

details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.calculation: A quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these animals would not be in the wild.'
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.
Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'Despite extensive preventive measures, five orangutans at Nyaru Menteng and six at Samboja Lestari were lost in 2024 due to health-related complications, including individuals that moved beyond pre-release islands and could no longer be monitored. These losses highlight the inherent challenges of managing large orangutan populations within semi-natural environments designed to foster independence.'
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.criteria: Bonus: Euthanasia/live-release data is disclosed. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found. | N/A: Not applicable for non-sheltering organisations.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

beneficiaries.location: Bukit Baka Bukit Raya National Park (TNBBBR), Indonesia

source.quote: In 2024, six orangutans were successfully released into their natural habitat
A total of five orangutans were rescued during the year.

beneficiaries.location: Nyaru Menteng and Samboja Lestari rehabilitation centers, Indonesia

source.quote: We recorded five births in rehabilitation during the year, four at Nyaru Menteng and one at Samboja Lestari.
the infants of Mentos and Inung are estimated to have been born in 2024

beneficiaries.location: Samboja Lestari and Nyaru Menteng rehabilitation centers, Indonesia

source.quote: In 2024, BOSF's medical teams managed 236 medical cases involving 208 orangutans.
Throughout the year, our medical teams treated 27 medical cases, primarily injuries and tumours

Conservation today requires more than returning orangutans to the forest. It demands the protection of ecosystems, the empowerment of Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities, and the integration of science into every decision.

metrics.context_qualifier: In 2024, into natural habitat

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these animals would not be in the wild.
In 2024, six orangutans were successfully released into their natural habitat

metrics.context_qualifier: Born to released orangutans Mentos and Inung

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without successful reintroduction and monitoring, these births would not be documented or potentially occur in the wild.
Based on body size assessments and developmental indicators, the infants of Mentos and Inung are estimated to have been born in 2024

metrics.context_qualifier: Threatened by habitat loss or illegal wildlife trade

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these animals would face severe threats.
A total of five orangutans were rescued during the year.

metrics.context_qualifier: Four at Nyaru Menteng and one at Samboja Lestari

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that these births are a result of successful care in rehabilitation.
We recorded five births in rehabilitation during the year, four at Nyaru Menteng and one at Samboja Lestari.

metrics.context_qualifier: Managed through 236 medical cases

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these animals would not receive treatment.
In 2024, BOSF's medical teams managed 236 medical cases involving 208 orangutans.

metrics.context_qualifier: Due to health-related complications (5 at Nyaru Menteng, 6 at Samboja Lestari)

source.quote: Not applicable, this is a negative outcome.
five orangutans at Nyaru Menteng and six at Samboja Lestari were lost in 2024 due to health-related complications

metrics.context_qualifier: 58 at Nyaru Menteng and 75 at Samboja Lestari

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these animals would not receive lifelong care.

By the end of 2024, 112 orangutans were living at the Samboja Lestari Rehabilitation Centre. Of these, 75 individuals were assessed as unreleasable... At Nyaru Menteng, 237 orangutans were under our care, with 58 classified as unreleasable.

metrics.context_qualifier: 27 males and 49 females at Samboja Lestari

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these animals would not receive care.
At Samboja Lestari, we continued caring for 76 sun bears (27 males and 49 females)

metrics.context_qualifier: By East Kalimantan BKSDA

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these animals would not be transferred.
In 2024, two new sun bears were transferred to our facility by the East Kalimantan BKSDA

metrics.context_qualifier: Primarily injuries and tumours

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these animals would not receive treatment.
Throughout the year, our medical teams treated 27 medical cases, primarily injuries and tumours

metrics.context_qualifier: Due to illness

source.quote: Not applicable, this is a negative outcome.
two sun bears were lost due to illness.

metrics.context_qualifier: In Samboja Lestari, through upkeep activities

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without maintenance, the forest growth would not continue optimally.
Throughout 2024, we maintained 244.74 hectares of previously land, carrying out essential upkeep activities such as replacement planting, weeding, and fertilisation to ensure continued forest growth.

metrics.context_qualifier: Across an additional 66.2 hectares in Samboja Lestari

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these trees would not be planted.
We also expanded our reforestation efforts significantly by planting 41,200 trees across an additional 66.2 hectares.

metrics.context_qualifier: To prevent illegal activities and reduce fire risks across the Mawas area

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without patrols, illegal activities and fire risks would be higher.
Throughout 2024, we conducted a total of 320 routine patrols to prevent illegal activities and reduce fire risks across the Mawas area.

metrics.context_qualifier: By Mawas team during 191 patrols in Kapuas and South Barito regencies

source.quote: Not applicable, this is a measure of detected negative activity.
our Mawas team carried out 191 patrols, during which they recorded 77 instances of illegal logging

metrics.context_qualifier: Mixed-species trees, detected by Mawas team

source.quote: Not applicable, this is a measure of detected negative activity.
These activities resulted in the illegal felling of 18,685 mixed-species trees

metrics.context_qualifier: Detected by Mawas team

source.quote: Not applicable, this is a measure of detected negative activity.
fire damage affecting 50 hectares of land.

metrics.context_qualifier: To support rapid fire suppression in high-risk zones

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these wells would not be constructed.

metrics.metric_name: Deep wells constructed for fire suppression

source.quote: We also constructed 32 deep wells to support rapid fire suppression

metrics.context_qualifier: At nurseries in Mangkatip, Mantangai Hulu, and Katimpun villages

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these seedlings would not be available for reforestation.

metrics.metric_name: Seedlings produced and maintained at nurseries

source.quote: production and maintenance of 474,399 seedlings at nurseries

metrics.context_qualifier: Across the Mawas area using community-based reforestation

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these areas would remain degraded.
we successfully reforested approximately 60 hectares across the Mawas area.

metrics.context_qualifier: Across 20 hectares in Block E and 40 hectares in Block A

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these seedlings would not be planted.
resulting in the placement of approximately 59,000 seedlings across these areas.

metrics.context_qualifier: In Block A and compartment D5 to improve hydrological conditions

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without intervention, these canals would continue to disrupt hydrological balance.
We blocked secondary canals in Block A and constructed 13 dams within the canal systems of compartment D5.

metrics.context_qualifier: From community-managed (beje) ponds, generating IDR 16,234,000 in income

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without support, this harvest would not occur or be as productive.
community-managed (beje) ponds harvested over 1,017 kg of fish, generating IDR 16,234,000 in income.

metrics.context_qualifier: Across Jakarta, Tangerang, Bekasi, Balikpapan, Samboja Lestari, and Palangka Raya by Orangutan Goes to School (OGTS) program

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without the program, these students would not be reached.
Our Orangutan Goes to School (OGTS) program... reached approximately 2,000 students across Jakarta, Tangerang, Bekasi, Balikpapan, Samboja Lestari, and Palangka Raya.

metrics.context_qualifier: From 40 schools in Samboja Lestari in the Students Goes to Nature programme

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without the program, these students would not participate.
Students Goes to Nature programme in Samboja Lestari, involving 40 schools and around 200 students

metrics.context_qualifier: In World Environment Day celebrations, including tree planting, competitions, and awareness campaigns

source.quote: Not explicitly stated, but implied that without the event, these participants would not be engaged.
Celebrations for World Environment Day engaged 329 participants and included tree planting, competitions, and awareness campaigns.

Despite extensive preventive measures, five orangutans at Nyaru Menteng and six at Samboja Lestari were lost in 2024 due to health-related complications, including individuals that moved beyond pre-release islands and could no longer be monitored.

Despite extensive preventive measures, five orangutans at Nyaru Menteng and six at Samboja Lestari were lost in 2024 due to health-related complications, including individuals that moved beyond pre-release islands and could no longer be monitored. These losses highlight the inherent challenges of managing large orangutan populations within semi-natural environments designed to foster independence.
